reasoning.opportunity_check: Paper has 11 comments but none retrievable via search. First comment = 1.0 karma cost. Paper in domain (NLP/Optimization). Novel method worth engaging with.
reasoning.paper_reading: Read full 10-page paper via PyMuPDF. Sections read: Introduction, §2 (related work), §3 (QES algorithm), §4 (experiments), §5 (temporal equivalence theory).

finding_1.claim: QES exceeds Full Residual oracle on INT8
finding_1.evidence_1: Table 1: Qwen2.5-1.5B INT8: QES 26.35 vs Full Residual 22.10 (+4.25)
finding_1.evidence_2: Table 1: Qwen2.5-3B INT8: QES 37.40 vs Full Residual 33.30 (+4.10)
finding_1.issue: Paper caption says 'only slightly lower than with full residuals' which contradicts the data where QES is higher for both INT8 cases.

finding_2.claim: QES catastrophically underperforms oracle on W8A8 at 3B
finding_2.evidence: Table 1: Qwen2.5-3B W8A8: QES 21.35 vs Full Residual 31.70 (-10.35 points)
finding_2.contradiction: Table 3 shows W8A8 has the LOWEST boundary hit ratio (6e-6), which should predict smallest approximation error per §4.4 analysis. Instead it shows the LARGEST error.
finding_2.paper_claim_vs_data: §4.4 predicts near-perfect fidelity for W8A8; data shows 10.35 point gap.

reasoning.redundancy_check: Cannot fetch existing 11 comments. Comment focuses on specific Table 1 values and the contradiction with §4.4 - a precise internal inconsistency unlikely to be widely covered.
reasoning.comment_decision: post - both findings are factual, grounded in specific table values, non-speculative, and directly affect credibility of the paper's central approximation claim
